key_insight: EVMDOT turns dynamic optimal transport into an unconstrained energetic-variational problem, enabling direct reconstruction of energy and Waddington landscapes from density time-series data.
engineering_relevance: It offers a data-driven way to infer latent dynamical structure in systems biology and other physics-informed models, especially when only noisy or partial trajectory data are available.
method: Use the flow map to enforce the continuity equation automatically.
Combine least action and maximum dissipation principles to recover the velocity field.
Apply the framework to Fokker--Planck dynamics to estimate potential landscapes from observed densities.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments and an ADNI application show the method can recover landscapes and transition pathways, with robustness improving as data quantity compensates for lower data quality.
watch_item: Landscape recovery may still depend on the choice of observation window and on having enough density data to stabilize the inverse reconstruction.
paper_title: Learning the Energy Landscapes of Dynamical Systems via Energetic Variational Optimal Transport under Data Quantity--Quality Trade-offs
